subject: Re: Extracting PyTom coordinates with
body:
Hi Dylan,

I only just had a look at your map/mask, but I would suggest making a
custom mask that follows the density as opposed to a spherical mask. I used
to struggle to get pytom-tm working and one of the eventual solutions that
helped was a better mask. I would just use relion_mask_create with
--extend_inimask and --width_soft_edge set to 2 and, judging from your
reference, --ini_threshold of 0.000174.

Cheers,

Mat

On Fri, 13 Feb 2026 at 08:04, Dylan Noone <dn13579.2013@my.bristol.ac.uk>
wrote:

> Examples of the shift attached (it was for sure my error with scaling)
>
> On Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:03 AM Dylan Noone <dn13579.2013@my.bristol.ac.uk>
> wrote:
>
>> Thanks all for the advice. I will work through the solutions suggested
>> and update on here.
>>
>> On Wed, Feb 11, 2026 at 10:50 PM Mathew McLaren <clazzy@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Dylan,
>>>
>>> Have you tried flipping your reference/mask and running again? Template
>>> matching is sensitive to handedness and while it can work with the wrong
>>> hand, it often works best if it's correct. If you haven't already, I would
>>> suggest trying the above tomogram with the opposite hand and see if it
>>> makes the points sharper. I've also found that sometimes you just need a
>>> reference that's generated from your data to get good results!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Mat
>>>
>>> On Wed, 11 Feb 2026 at 17:37, 'Dylan Noone' via Warp <
>>> warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> and mask.....
>>>>
>>>> On Wednesday, February 11, 2026 at 5:36:37 PM UTC Dylan Noone wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hey Thomas,
>>>>>
>>>>> Thanks for your response, especially sharing your command. I think I
>>>>> am not doign something weird in that step based off this.
>>>>>
>>>>> So I think the mismatch in coordaintes may have been from a mistake on
>>>>> my end when scaling them to view them in IMOD!
>>>>>
>>>>> However, I think something is still going wrong as my scores in Napari
>>>>> look really weird (images of tomo and scores from PyTom). I have tried a
>>>>> number of different arguements and tweaks with the commands, I was if there
>>>>> was something wrong with my template (also attached)?
>>>>>
>>>>> Thank you,
>>>>> Dylan
>>>>>
